title: The Estoril Procession
theme: A Trophy race that ended at lunchtime on day one: Gregg Williams seized the lead on the back nine of round one and was never headed, even while spraying double-figure scores around the Lisbon Coast. The only live contest was for the Green Jacket, where Mullin and Jon Baker traded haymakers; the only certainty was Stuart Neumann at the bottom of everything.
opening_hook: Open with the absurdity of the final margin — Williams +12 on the Trophy while carding a 10 at Penha Longa, a 10 at Quinta and an 8 at Oitavos — then flash back to round one, where the race was effectively decided.
foreshadow: Williams takes outright Trophy lead at R1 H10 and the field never gets within touching distance again
Mullin's narrow R1 Jacket lead foreshadows a four-round duel with Jon Baker
Neumann drops to last on R1 H4 and stays there for 68 holes
Quinta da Marinha's back nine eats everyone — set up the carnage that will return in R3

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Wire-to-wire. Williams went outright on R1 H10 (the only lead change of the tournament), stretched the gap to 11 after Penha Longa on the back of a 41-point round, survived a wobbly 29 at Quinta in R3 (lead briefly down to 5) and then closed with another 41 at Oitavos for a 12-shot win on 149. Remarkably, he won doing this while making a quintuple-bogey 10 in R2, another 10 in R3, and three further quadruple bogeys.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: The only real contest. Mullin led after R1 by one and after R2 by six, then collapsed to a 26 in R3 at Quinta — his worst round to date — letting Jon Baker take the lead by two. At Oitavos he reclaimed outright leadership at the 2nd hole and rebuilt the cushion to win by six on +77.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Never not last. Neumann was bottom from the first hole, ceded the slot to Meller for a single hole when Henry made a 10 at the Quinta 3rd, then reclaimed it at the 4th and stayed there for the remaining 68 holes. Defining moments: a 29-shots-dropped meltdown across holes 7-15 of R1, an 11 at the Quinta 10th in R3, and a stretch of five quintuple bogeys across the week. Final tally: 100 points.

headline_candidates: Williams Plants the Flag at Quinta
Neumann Bleeds 29 on the Back Nine
rounds.chosen_headline: Williams Plants the Flag at Quinta
rounds.angle: On a brand new Trent Jones Sr. layout, Williams's birdie-laced surge through holes 8-11 puts him outright on the Trophy at H10 — the only lead change he'll ever need. Behind him, Neumann posts a 17 and Meller briefly dips to last before Stuart reclaims the basement.
On the dramatic Penha Longa mountain layout, Williams cards a quintuple-bogey 10 at the 12th and somehow extends his Trophy lead to 11. Mullin, meanwhile, opens daylight on the Jacket with a 14.

headline_candidates: The Day Everyone Lost Their Ball
Jon Baker's Cameo at the Top
rounds.angle: The return to Quinta da Marinha turns into a collective car-crash: Williams's worst round of the week (29) still preserves a 5-shot Trophy lead, Mullin's career-worst 26 hands Jon Baker a two-shot Jacket lead, Alex Baker takes a 10 at the 8th, Neumann makes an 11 at the 10th plus three more quintuples, and the round bleeds shots from every card.

headline_candidates: Mullin Reclaims the Jacket at the 2nd
Meller's Six-Hole Nightmare by the Sea
rounds.chosen_headline: Mullin Reclaims the Jacket at the 2nd
rounds.angle: On Portugal's premier links, Mullin retakes the Gross lead outright at the 2nd hole and never loses it, winning by six. Williams seals the Trophy by 12 with a closing 41. Meller bleeds 22 shots in the opening six holes including a 10 at the 4th. Neumann completes a wire-to-wire Spoon.

players.arc: Took the Trophy lead at R1 H10 and never let go; won by 12 despite shipping a quintuple bogey in three different rounds — the dominant champion who scored big in spite of himself.
Led the Jacket from R1, lost it briefly after a career-worst 26 at Quinta in R3, reclaimed it on the 2nd at Oitavos and ran out a six-shot winner.
The only man to interrupt the leaderboards — took the Jacket lead through R3 before Mullin reeled him back in at Oitavos; settled for runner-up by six.
Trophy runner-up at 12 back — a quietly consistent week pockmarked by a sextuple-bogey 10 at the Quinta 8th and a steady drip of quadruple bogeys.
Briefly last on R1 H3 after a 10 at the par-5, recovered, then suffered an Oitavos opener for the ages: 22 shots dropped in six holes, including another 10.
Last from R1 H4 to the final putt: 17-point opener, an 11 at Quinta in R3, five quintuple bogeys for the week and a 100-point Spoon by some distance.

venue_notes: TEG's second visit to the Lisbon Coast (after a previous trip), but three of the four courses are new to TEG: Quinta da Marinha (Trent Jones Sr., parkland, hosting twice — R1 and R3), Penha Longa (Trent Jones Jr., dramatic Sintra mountain parkland) and Oitavos Dunes (Arthur Hills, Atlantic links — Portugal's premier course). Use the Trent Jones father-and-son axis as colour; lean on Quinta's elevated targets and bunkering when describing the R1/R3 carnage, the Sintra hills for R2, and the rugged Atlantic links for the R4 finale.
